title: R-1 parser-instance reset on throw is INSUFFICIENT — WASM module-level corruption persists across new Parser() boundaries
impact: Forces remediation toward skip-list (R-3) + process quarantine (R-1'); per-instance reset path closed.

title: Variant B (sh-excluded) — bash IS necessary trigger; non-bash cohort is parser-clean indefinitely
impact: Locks bash as necessary AND sufficient trigger. Skip-list bash-by-default exclusion eliminates B2 by construction.

title: Variant A (sh-only) — bash poisons bash without other-language interleaving; iso-corruption-budget is ~80 B1 throws
impact: Refines mechanism: trigger is bash-B1-throw count (~80), not parse count. Hardens F-5.3 from medium to high confidence.

title: Phase-2 minimum-cost remediation: skip-list (R-3) primary, process quarantine (R-1') escalation
impact: Final Phase-2 hierarchy locked for iter-7 synthesis: R-3a (skip-list table) + R-3b (bash-by-default) + R-1' (process quarantine sentinel) + R-4 Phase-3 (web-tree-sitter@0.26.x bump).

title: Proposed catch-block diff at tree-sitter-parser.ts:741-756 — ~7 LOC for skip-list write + quarantine sentinel
impact: Concrete diff target ready for iter-7 synthesis plan.md fragment.

title: Variant A revealed setLanguage B2 escape path; production catch already covers it
impact: Pre-empts a Phase-2 follow-up concern; no code change required.

note: Content matters only via bash-B1 fingerprint.
run-all.sh family is reliable B1+B2 progenitor; standalone <50-LOC bash extraction deferred to post-research.
